channel.title: Dustin Dikes - Week Notes
channel.description: Updates of the things I have done each week

item.title: Week Notes 2026-04
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-01-19 - 2026-01-25

I've followed Noisy Deadline's blog for a while now, and I always loved seeing her week notes. Week notes are an indie web concept where you publish a post at the end of each week detailing things you did. I decided I'd document my studying within the infosec space through week notes.

📚 Read

  • HTB How to Become a Pentester This was a nice intro guide on how to get started in pentesting. It actually inspired me to do these week notes. Having a blog showing continual learning should be helpful to set me apart when I'm looking for a job. I'm going to also start doing more CTFs and things like HTB machines to build my skills and to post write-ups for them.

🎥 Watched

  • Nahamsec - This simple vulnerability was worth $70,000
  • Talking Sasquach - BLEShark Nano: Pocket Sized WiFi Security Testing!

🎧 Listened

  • The OSINT Podcast - Ep 2
    • Paste Gmail address in sheets, hover over it, may give you name and confirm it's an account
    • Url for confirming valid Gmail account with calendar (src): https[:]//calendar[.]google[.]com/calendar/u/0/embed?src=youremail@example[.]com
  • Dark Diaries Podcast - Ep 166: Maxie This was a fun episode. I always love hearing stories of physical pen tests. The underwater data center bit was interesting as well.

👨‍💻 Hacked:

  • Completed HTB File Inclusion Module
  • Set up this blog!

🏫 School

  • Studying for the CompTIA Project+ certificate
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-01-19 - 2026-01-25

I've followed Noisy Deadline's blog for a while now, and I always loved seeing her week notes. Week notes are an indie web concept where you publish a post at the end of each week detailing things you did. I decided I'd document my studying within the infosec space through week notes.

📚 Read

  • HTB How to Become a Pentester This was a nice intro guide on how to get started in pentesting. It actually inspired me to do these week notes. Having a blog showing continual learning should be helpful to set me apart when I'm looking for a job. I'm going to also start doing more CTFs and things like HTB machines to build my skills and to post write-ups for them.

🎥 Watched

  • Nahamsec - This simple vulnerability was worth $70,000
  • Talking Sasquach - BLEShark Nano: Pocket Sized WiFi Security Testing!

🎧 Listened

  • The OSINT Podcast - Ep 2
    • Paste Gmail address in sheets, hover over it, may give you name and confirm it's an account
    • Url for confirming valid Gmail account with calendar (src): https[:]//calendar[.]google[.]com/calendar/u/0/embed?src=youremail@example[.]com
  • Dark Diaries Podcast - Ep 166: Maxie This was a fun episode. I always love hearing stories of physical pen tests. The underwater data center bit was interesting as well.

👨‍💻 Hacked:

  • Completed HTB File Inclusion Module
  • Set up this blog!

🏫 School

  • Studying for the CompTIA Project+ certificate

item.title: Week Notes 2026-05
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-01-25 - 2026-01-31

📚 Read

  • Beauty in Destruction: Exploring Malware's Impact Through Art I didn't know Malware based at exhibits existed. That is something that's right up my alley. I've always been very into art and computers and have a particular affinity for intersection of technology and art.

🎥 Watched

  • Nahamsec - How to Become a Top Bug Bounty Hunter in 2026
  • Talking Sasquach - This 5GHz WiFi Device is INSANE

🎧 Listened

  • OSINT Podcast episode 3 - The Dark Side of OSINT

👨‍💻 Hacked

  • I've been working through the OSINT Newsletter CTF

🏫 School

  • Studying for the CompTIA Project+ certificate
item.content:encoded:

📚 Read

  • Talking Sasquach - This 5GHz WiFi Device is INSANE

🎧 Listened

  • OSINT Podcast episode 3 - The Dark Side of OSINT

👨‍💻 Hacked

  • I've been working through the OSINT Newsletter CTF

🏫 School

  • Studying for the CompTIA Project+ certificate

item.title: Week Notes 2026-06
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-02-02 - 2026-02-08

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - Hacking a Windows Web Application
    • Shortscan
    • FFUF flag -ac for color
  • NahamSec - No Code Bug Bounty Automation Using n8n
  • NahamSec - Best Hacking Tools For 2026
    • Proxy: Caido, BurpSuite, Zapp
    • Subdomain enumeration: subfinder, amass
    • Fuzzing: FFUF, gobuster
    • Wayback machine: waymore
    • Crawler: katana
    • Blind XSS scan: easyXSS
    • Vulnerability Scanner: Nuclei
    • To watch: NahamSec recon methodology video

🎧 Listened

  • Darknet Diaries - EP 168: LoD
  • Darknet Diaries - EP 169: MoD

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Project+

🆕 Misc

  • installed Omarchy. I have previously set up my own system with i3, but after watching the Omarchy demo video I knew this would be perfect for me. My workflows align very well with those of the creator, so I figured I'd jump ship and give it a go. So far I'm loving it!
  • Coached at the Spokane Cyber Cup CTF! It was really fun, and we had a great turnout of talented student hackers!
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-02-02 - 2026-02-08

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - Hacking a Windows Web Application
    • Shortscan
    • FFUF flag -ac for color
  • NahamSec - No Code Bug Bounty Automation Using n8n
  • NahamSec - Best Hacking Tools For 2026
    • Proxy: Caido, BurpSuite, Zapp
    • Subdomain enumeration: subfinder, amass
    • Fuzzing: FFUF, gobuster
    • Wayback machine: waymore
    • Crawler: katana
    • Blind XSS scan: easyXSS
    • Vulnerability Scanner: Nuclei
    • To watch: NahamSec recon methodology video

🎧 Listened

  • Darknet Diaries - EP 168: LoD
  • Darknet Diaries - EP 169: MoD

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Project+

🆕 Misc

  • installed Omarchy. I have previously set up my own system with i3, but after watching the Omarchy demo video I knew this would be perfect for me. My workflows align very well with those of the creator, so I figured I'd jump ship and give it a go. So far I'm loving it!
  • Coached at the Spokane Cyber Cup CTF! It was really fun, and we had a great turnout of talented student hackers!

item.title: Week Notes 2026-07
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-02-09 - 2026-02-15

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • Working through HTB Pentest in a Nutshell
  • Watching: Johnny Mnemonic

📚 Read

  • Jailbreaking LLMs: A Comprehensive Guide (With Examples)

🎥 Watched

  • Finished Cyberpunk: Edgerunners This was a great anime. I loved the cyberpunk feel. Very Neuromancer. I've not played/read anything else from the Cyberpunk 2077 franchise, but I definitely want to dive deeper into this world!

👨‍💻 Hacked

  • GraySwan Arena - Safeguards Wave 0 (Red Team) - I managed to break the LLM for the first time, so that was exciting!
  • Snyk Fetch the Flag 2026 CTF - This was a fun CTF! I worked on it with two other Defcon 509 members. We successfully completed all flags (10 of them) in the digital forensics section. This challenge provided a very large pcap file and a list of questions where the answers are the flag. You had to find the answers based on what you could find in the pcap file. We also tried our hands at the web challenges and AI challenges, but didn't manage to get any flags there. Overall it was really fun doing a CTF with a team!

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Project+
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-02-09 - 2026-02-15

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • Working through HTB Pentest in a Nutshell
  • Watching: Johnny Mnemonic

📚 Read

  • Jailbreaking LLMs: A Comprehensive Guide (With Examples)

🎥 Watched

  • Finished Cyberpunk: Edgerunners This was a great anime. I loved the cyberpunk feel. Very Neuromancer. I've not played/read anything else from the Cyberpunk 2077 franchise, but I definitely want to dive deeper into this world!

👨‍💻 Hacked

  • GraySwan Arena - Safeguards Wave 0 (Red Team) - I managed to break the LLM for the first time, so that was exciting!
  • Snyk Fetch the Flag 2026 CTF - This was a fun CTF! I worked on it with two other Defcon 509 members. We successfully completed all flags (10 of them) in the digital forensics section. This challenge provided a very large pcap file and a list of questions where the answers are the flag. You had to find the answers based on what you could find in the pcap file. We also tried our hands at the web challenges and AI challenges, but didn't manage to get any flags there. Overall it was really fun doing a CTF with a team!

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Project+

item.title: Week Notes 2026-08
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-02-16 - 2026-02-22

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • Working through HTB Pentest in a Nutshell

📚 Read

  • Shellsharks - Getting Into Information Security - This was a very informative post and I'd highly suggest it to anyone looking to get into infosec! Reading through it gave me a million other things to look into, which is a good problem to have! This post also made me feel a little better about my choice to get a Cybersecurity degree and the certificates that come with it (mostly CompTIA). It can be easy to feel I'm wasting my time and money when people are often saying you don't need degrees/certs to get into security.
  • Shellsharks - Owning My Own Social - Interesting post about hosting a single user mastodon server. I've tried this before and it was complicated. For now I'm happy with my current instance (run by someone I know IRL). I think I may look into posting on my site and syndicating out to mastodon though.

🎥 Watched

  • Johnny Mnemonic - I've never seen or read this, but I've meant to for a while now. This movie is very dated and very amusing just like all old hacker/cyberpunk movies. Definitely enjoyed it.
  • John Hammond - TiKTok needs to fix this vulnerability
  • John Hammond - Russia is hacking zero-days again - It was really interesting watching him do some command line magic to analyze the malware file! I'd love to look more into malware analysis someday.

🎧 Listened

  • Critical Thinking Podcast - Episode 160: Cloudflare Zero-days & Mail Unsubscribing for XSS
  • Critical Thinking Podcast - Episode 161: Cross-Consumer Attacks & DTMF Tone Exfil
  • Darknet Diaries - EP 170: Phrack

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Project+

🗒️ Published

  • Snyk Fetch the Flag 2026 CTF Writeup
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-02-16 - 2026-02-22

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • Working through HTB Pentest in a Nutshell

📚 Read

  • Shellsharks - Getting Into Information Security - This was a very informative post and I'd highly suggest it to anyone looking to get into infosec! Reading through it gave me a million other things to look into, which is a good problem to have! This post also made me feel a little better about my choice to get a Cybersecurity degree and the certificates that come with it (mostly CompTIA). It can be easy to feel I'm wasting my time and money when people are often saying you don't need degrees/certs to get into security.
  • Shellsharks - Owning My Own Social - Interesting post about hosting a single user mastodon server. I've tried this before and it was complicated. For now I'm happy with my current instance (run by someone I know IRL). I think I may look into posting on my site and syndicating out to mastodon though.

🎥 Watched

  • Johnny Mnemonic - I've never seen or read this, but I've meant to for a while now. This movie is very dated and very amusing just like all old hacker/cyberpunk movies. Definitely enjoyed it.
  • John Hammond - TiKTok needs to fix this vulnerability
  • John Hammond - Russia is hacking zero-days again - It was really interesting watching him do some command line magic to analyze the malware file! I'd love to look more into malware analysis someday.

🎧 Listened

  • Critical Thinking Podcast - Episode 160: Cloudflare Zero-days & Mail Unsubscribing for XSS
  • Critical Thinking Podcast - Episode 161: Cross-Consumer Attacks & DTMF Tone Exfil
  • Darknet Diaries - EP 170: Phrack

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Project+

🗒️ Published

  • Snyk Fetch the Flag 2026 CTF Writeup

item.title: Week Notes 2026-09
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-02-23 - 2026-03-01

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - I Hacked My First AI Chatbot - This video went into a couple of the AI challenges from the SNYK CTF I completed in recently. I don't think I attempted the first one, which is unfortunate because I may have been able to figure that one out. I did try the vibecoding one, without success and or was interesting to see the solution for that one. I did manage to get the username, but I never managed to get it to give me the password. I learned a lot from this video regarding system prompt override.
  • NahamSec - Can I Replace AI With My Recon Methodology?

🏫 School

  • Passed CompTIA Project+
  • Passed Linux Professional Institute (LPI) Linux Essentials
  • Passed Python for Automation class
  • Studying for CompTIA CySA+

🆕 Misc

  • Went to the Defcon 509 meetup. We had a great talk on Zero Trust Architecture! Also someone may have turned off all the TVs with a flipper zero 👀
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-02-23 - 2026-03-01

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - I Hacked My First AI Chatbot - This video went into a couple of the AI challenges from the SNYK CTF I completed in recently. I don't think I attempted the first one, which is unfortunate because I may have been able to figure that one out. I did try the vibecoding one, without success and or was interesting to see the solution for that one. I did manage to get the username, but I never managed to get it to give me the password. I learned a lot from this video regarding system prompt override.
  • NahamSec - Can I Replace AI With My Recon Methodology?

🏫 School

  • Passed CompTIA Project+
  • Passed Linux Professional Institute (LPI) Linux Essentials
  • Passed Python for Automation class
  • Studying for CompTIA CySA+

🆕 Misc

  • Went to the Defcon 509 meetup. We had a great talk on Zero Trust Architecture! Also someone may have turned off all the TVs with a flipper zero 👀

item.title: Week Notes 2026-10
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-03-02 - 2026-03-08

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - 10+ Daily Essentials As An Ethical Hacker
  • NahamSec - A $40,000 Remote Code Execution (Walkthrough)
  • NahamSec - This Hackers Hacked, Tracked & Unlocked Every Subaru

🎧 Listened

  • Critical Thinking - Episode 158: 10hr Marathon Hack-Along Recap + $300k Client-side Bugs

🏫 School

  • Passed CompTIA CySA+
  • Passed Cloud Security class
  • Recieved CompTIA Security Analytics Professional – CSAP stackable cert
  • Studying for CompTIA Pentest+
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-03-02 - 2026-03-08

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - 10+ Daily Essentials As An Ethical Hacker
  • NahamSec - A $40,000 Remote Code Execution (Walkthrough)
  • NahamSec - This Hackers Hacked, Tracked & Unlocked Every Subaru

🎧 Listened

  • Critical Thinking - Episode 158: 10hr Marathon Hack-Along Recap + $300k Client-side Bugs

🏫 School

  • Passed CompTIA CySA+
  • Passed Cloud Security class
  • Recieved CompTIA Security Analytics Professional – CSAP stackable cert
  • Studying for CompTIA Pentest+

item.title: Week Notes 2026-11
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-03-09 - 2026-03-15

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

📚 Read

  • disrupting a cryptocurrency phishing scam abusing telegram bots - This post was covered in the John Hammond video below, but I wanted to read through it myself. I find the hack back/disruption the author chose to pursue to be fantastic and I'm glad there are people out there trying to actively prevent cyber crimes. Also it's fortunate to find that nobody actually entered their recovery key, so there were no victims.

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - Was This Vulnerability Worth $15,000?
  • John Hammond - Hardware Hacking 101: with a custom physical kit!
  • John Hammond - crypto scammers phish with physical mail - Using physical mail as the attack vector is an interesting deviation from the norm. I love seeing videos/posts like this where they go through the tactics of threat actors. It's very insightful.
  • John Hammond - h?ckers are globbing
  • Talking Sasquach - I Waited 2 Years for This – Monstatek M1 Reality Check
  • Talking Sasquach - 5 Genius Tools That Turn a WiFi Pager Into a Blue Team Arsenal
  • John Hammond - NahamSec Teaches Me Bug Bounty Basics

🎧 Listened

  • Darknet Diaries - EP 171: Melody Fraud - This episode was really interesting. I guess I knew there was streaming platform fraud, but I didn't know it ran so deep with such big payouts and when enabling money cleaning/laundering.
  • Critical Thinking - Episode 164: Tommy DeVoss: From Black Hat to Bug Bounty LEGEND - This episode was really fun. I loved listening to the origin story. It was very reminiscent of the LoD and MoD episodes of Darknet Diaries. The Internet was wild back in the late 90s early 00s.

🏫 School

  • Studying for Comptia Pentest+
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-03-09 - 2026-03-15

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia

📚 Read

  • disrupting a cryptocurrency phishing scam abusing telegram bots - This post was covered in the John Hammond video below, but I wanted to read through it myself. I find the hack back/disruption the author chose to pursue to be fantastic and I'm glad there are people out there trying to actively prevent cyber crimes. Also it's fortunate to find that nobody actually entered their recovery key, so there were no victims.

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - Was This Vulnerability Worth $15,000?
  • John Hammond - Hardware Hacking 101: with a custom physical kit!
  • John Hammond - crypto scammers phish with physical mail - Using physical mail as the attack vector is an interesting deviation from the norm. I love seeing videos/posts like this where they go through the tactics of threat actors. It's very insightful.
  • John Hammond - h?ckers are globbing
  • Talking Sasquach - I Waited 2 Years for This – Monstatek M1 Reality Check
  • Talking Sasquach - 5 Genius Tools That Turn a WiFi Pager Into a Blue Team Arsenal
  • John Hammond - NahamSec Teaches Me Bug Bounty Basics

🎧 Listened

  • Darknet Diaries - EP 171: Melody Fraud - This episode was really interesting. I guess I knew there was streaming platform fraud, but I didn't know it ran so deep with such big payouts and when enabling money cleaning/laundering.
  • Critical Thinking - Episode 164: Tommy DeVoss: From Black Hat to Bug Bounty LEGEND - This episode was really fun. I loved listening to the origin story. It was very reminiscent of the LoD and MoD episodes of Darknet Diaries. The Internet was wild back in the late 90s early 00s.

🏫 School

  • Studying for Comptia Pentest+

item.title: Week Notes 2026-12
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-03-16 - 2026-03-23

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • HackTheBox: Pentest in a Nutshell

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - BECOMING AN AI HACKER (Episode 01)
  • John Hammond - ChatGPT For The Dark Web - I surprisingly really enjoyed this video given that I'm typically against vibecoding. This stems from my many years of experience as a software engineer and knowing that throwing vibe coded software into production without heavily vetting the code is a dangerous game. That being said, I think using it as a security researcher could be very helpful for creating tools for your own research. Not tools to publish, but just getting something up and running quickly to achieve the thing you need to do. The actual app created in this video seemed useful and functional and it was interesting seeing how something like this is done with AI.
  • John Hammond - thousands of Google API keys exposed

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Pentest+
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-03-16 - 2026-03-23

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • HackTheBox: Pentest in a Nutshell

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - BECOMING AN AI HACKER (Episode 01)
  • John Hammond - ChatGPT For The Dark Web - I surprisingly really enjoyed this video given that I'm typically against vibecoding. This stems from my many years of experience as a software engineer and knowing that throwing vibe coded software into production without heavily vetting the code is a dangerous game. That being said, I think using it as a security researcher could be very helpful for creating tools for your own research. Not tools to publish, but just getting something up and running quickly to achieve the thing you need to do. The actual app created in this video seemed useful and functional and it was interesting seeing how something like this is done with AI.
  • John Hammond - thousands of Google API keys exposed

🏫 School

  • Studying for CompTIA Pentest+

item.title: Week Notes 2026-14
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-03-30 - 2026-04-05

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • HackTheBox: Pentest in a Nutshell

📚 Read

  • Hackaday - Open Graphics Card Powers Cyberpunk "Laptop" This screen and projected keyboard look really cool! I'd love to build some sort of cyberdeck for fun someday. Maybe one with useful hacking tools or just running Kali.

🎥 Watched

  • Talking Sasquach - Is This the Budget Flipper Zero? HaleHound Firmware - I'm definitely gonna need to get a CYD sometime to mess around with. This firmware seems really cool/useful and I'm curious what other firmware is out there related to hacking tools.
  • NahamSec - I Earned $2M Hacking. Here's Everything I Know
  • Talking Sasquach - The Gadgets That Taught Me Everything About Cybersecurity
  • Tragic mistake... Anthropic leaks Claude’s source code

🏫 School

  • Passed CompTIA Pentest+!
  • Working on final capstone project
item.content:encoded:

🎥 Watched

  • Talking Sasquach - Is This the Budget Flipper Zero? HaleHound Firmware - I'm definitely gonna need to get a CYD sometime to mess around with. This firmware seems really cool/useful and I'm curious what other firmware is out there related to hacking tools.
  • NahamSec - I Earned $2M Hacking. Here's Everything I Know
  • Talking Sasquach - The Gadgets That Taught Me Everything About Cybersecurity
  • Tragic mistake... Anthropic leaks Claude’s source code

🏫 School

  • Passed CompTIA Pentest+!
  • Working on final capstone project

item.title: Week Notes 2026-16
item.dc:creator: Dustin Dikes
item.description:
📅 Week of 2026-04-13 - 2026-04-19

I didn't publish a week notes post for last week (2026-15), as it was spring break and I did nothing related to school or infosec. Some needed time away from a computer 🙂

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • HackTheBox: Pentest in a Nutshell
  • WGU Cyber Challenge Range

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - An AI Hacker Showed Me How to Exfil Data in Zero Clicks
  • NahamSec - Is AI Killing Bug Bounty?
  • Marcus Hutchins - Is Cybersecurity Over?
  • John Hammond - HUGE AI-powered Microsoft Account phishing campaign
  • John Hammond - How Teenage Hackers Hijack the Internet (with Joe Tidy!)

🏫 School

  • Working on final capstone project
item.content:encoded:
📅 Week of 2026-04-13 - 2026-04-19

I didn't publish a week notes post for last week (2026-15), as it was spring break and I did nothing related to school or infosec. Some needed time away from a computer 🙂

🕒 Current

  • Reading: Hunting Cyber Criminals by Vinny Troia
  • HackTheBox: Pentest in a Nutshell
  • WGU Cyber Challenge Range

🎥 Watched

  • NahamSec - An AI Hacker Showed Me How to Exfil Data in Zero Clicks
  • NahamSec - Is AI Killing Bug Bounty?
  • Marcus Hutchins - Is Cybersecurity Over?
  • John Hammond - HUGE AI-powered Microsoft Account phishing campaign
  • John Hammond - How Teenage Hackers Hijack the Internet (with Joe Tidy!)

🏫 School

  • Working on final capstone project
